Phase-43.NAME: MTProto Admin Analytics UX, Abuse Alerts and 90-day IP Observability
Phase-43.purpose: Upgrade MTProto admin analytics into a compact operational console with abuse notifications, 90-day admin-only IP history, user search, graphs, proxy CPU/RAM metrics, and bounded storage retention.

scope.in: Remove visible Recent events, add abuse alert inbox, collect admin-only encrypted raw client IP observations for 90 days, add user/proxy search, current/last/all IP views, top-user expansion, timeseries graphs, runtime CPU/RAM metrics, auto-refresh, storage-budget retention, and confirmation-safe admin actions.
scope.out: Telegram chat/channel/message visibility, Telegram account inspection, payload inspection, automatic punishment without admin review, deploy/install script redesign, and exposing raw IPs to users or logs.

privacy-boundary.rule-1: MTProto payload, Telegram chats, channels, messages, contacts, and Telegram user ids remain out of scope.
privacy-boundary.rule-2: Raw client IP is allowed only for authenticated admins, encrypted at rest, scoped to investigation screens, and retained for 90 days.
privacy-boundary.rule-3: Raw IP must not be copied into application logs, audit details, non-admin APIs, frontend bundles, user responses, or smoke output.
privacy-boundary.rule-4: Unknown or untrusted IP source must be shown as source_ip_unavailable rather than inferred.

retention-policy.raw-events: Detailed MTProto usage events are kept briefly for debugging and rollup rebuilds.
retention-policy.sessions: Session summaries and exact encrypted IP observations are kept for the requested three-month investigation window.
retention-policy.hourly-rollups: Hourly graph data is kept for three-month admin history.
retention-policy.daily-rollups: Daily traffic, connection, duration, and error aggregates are kept for annual trend review.
retention-policy.monthly-rollups: Monthly aggregates are kept for long-horizon service sizing.
retention-policy.alerts: Abuse alerts and review states are kept longer than raw IP evidence for operational accountability.

steps.step-1: Update telemetry privacy and retention contract so raw IP is admin-only, encrypted, and retained for 90 days while raw usage events remain bounded to 30 days.
steps.step-2: Design Alembic-backed IP observation storage with encrypted raw IP, HMAC hash, prefix, current_active, first_seen, last_seen, and assignment/user indexes.
steps.step-3: Extend KPprotoN runtime telemetry and backend ingestion to carry trustworthy client IP and proxy process resource metrics when available.
steps.step-4: Record IP observations from handshake, active connection, and close events without logging raw IP strings.
steps.step-5: Add timeseries aggregation for traffic, connections, active connections, errors, and duration across hour/day buckets.
steps.step-6: Upgrade abuse detection to create deduplicated high/critical admin alerts while keeping observe-first behavior.
steps.step-7: Expose admin-only APIs for alerts, user/proxy search, user usage detail, all IPs, current IPs, last IP, sessions, timeseries, resource metrics, and storage budget.
steps.step-8: Add confirmation-safe alert actions: acknowledge, resolve, disable proxy, and optional TTL-bound IP block with audit events.
steps.step-9: Rebuild the admin MTProto analytics UI: remove Recent events, add Overview/Users/Abuse/Settings, longer Top users, user detail drawer, graphs, resource tiles, and compact mobile layout.
steps.step-10: Add auto-refresh with separate intervals for summary/resources/alerts, top users, graph data, and slow-changing settings.
steps.step-11: Schedule abuse scan, rollup rebuild, raw event prune, IP observation prune, and storage-budget health checks.
steps.step-12: Run backend, migration, runtime, UI build, static redaction, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and optional live analytics smoke after operator deploy.

data-flow.flow-1: Telegram client -> RU SNI router -> DE KPprotoN runtime -> runtime emits SNI, event type, counters, optional client IP, and resource metrics.
data-flow.flow-2: Backend telemetry ingestion -> M-054 raw events/state/sessions -> M-061 encrypted IP observations -> M-056 analytics/timeseries.
data-flow.flow-3: M-056 abuse classifier -> M-060 deduplicated alert -> M-057 admin API -> M-058 compact alert inbox and user detail drawer.
data-flow.flow-4: Admin reviewed action -> M-047 proxy disable or TTL IP block -> M-026 audit record -> M-060 alert resolved state.

mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-1: Clear abuse: one proxy crosses IP/concurrency thresholds, M-056 records high severity, M-060 creates one open alert, M-058 shows a badge, admin opens user detail, reviews 90-day IP history, and disables the proxy with confirmation.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-2: Normal mobile roaming: a user changes networks but remains below tuned thresholds, M-061 records IP history, no high alert is generated, and the admin can still see last/current IP context.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-3: Storage pressure: raw events older than 30 days and exact IP observations older than 90 days are pruned, while hourly/daily/monthly rollups still power three-month and older trend graphs.

phase-gates.gate-1: Recent events are not visible in the admin MTProto analytics panel.
phase-gates.gate-2: Admin can search a user/proxy and see issued_at, last_seen_at, all IPs for 90 days, current IPs, last IP, sessions, traffic, duration, and errors.
phase-gates.gate-3: Admin receives visible high/critical abuse alerts and can acknowledge or resolve them.
phase-gates.gate-4: Proxy disable and IP block actions require explicit confirmation and audit records.
phase-gates.gate-5: Graphs and runtime CPU/RAM metrics auto-refresh without causing noisy re-layout on mobile.
phase-gates.gate-6: Retention jobs bound storage growth while preserving three-month operational history.
phase-gates.gate-7: Redaction gates prove no raw IP leaks outside explicit admin investigation APIs and no secrets/tg links are exposed.

risks.risk-1: Runtime telemetry may not always expose a trustworthy client IP; the implementation must show source_ip_unavailable instead of guessing.
risks.risk-2: Raw IP observability increases privacy and data-protection responsibility; encryption, audit scope, and retention must be strict.
risks.risk-3: IP blocking can affect unrelated users behind NAT or mobile carriers; proxy disable remains the safer default action.
risks.risk-4: Telemetry volume can grow quickly; retention and rollups are release gates, not optional cleanup.
risks.risk-5: Exact per-user byte attribution under concurrent SNI activity remains limited until deeper data-plane hooks are proven live.

verification-design.gate-1: Encrypted IP storage must decrypt only through admin-only code paths and prune exact observations after 90 days.
verification-design.gate-2: Abuse alert creation must dedupe, notify, acknowledge, resolve, and never auto-block.
verification-design.gate-3: Admin search, detail, top users, graphs, resource metrics, and auto-refresh must work on compact desktop and mobile layouts.
verification-design.gate-4: Disable proxy and IP block actions must require confirmation and write audit-safe events.
verification-design.gate-5: Telemetry ingestion, rollups, runtime metrics, and retention must not break Phase-41 MTProto availability.

commands.command: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-43.xml docs/modules/M-060.xml docs/modules/M-061.xml docs/verification/V-M-060.xml docs/verification/V-M-061.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/requirements.xml

cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_ip_observability.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_alerts.py tests/test_mtproto_usage_telemetry.py tests/test_mtproto_analytics_service.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_analytics_api.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_db_migrations.py -v

evidence-matrix.module-local: M-061 encryption/retention, M-060 alert lifecycle, M-056 timeseries/abuse, M-057 admin-only APIs, and M-058 compact UI smokes are verified independently first.
evidence-matrix.wave-level: M-054/M-055/M-056 prove telemetry -> rollup -> analytics; M-060/M-047/M-026 prove alert -> action -> audit; M-057/M-058 prove API -> UI contract.
evidence-matrix.phase-level: Phase-43 closes only after redaction, retention, mobile UI, storage budget, and no-regression MTProto availability checks pass.
evidence-matrix.live-optional: After clean deploy, operator can validate live alert visibility, graph refresh, resource metrics, and Telegram proxy continuity with redacted evidence.
